definition.name: cyclic_vector
definition.definition: Let π : A → B(H) be a *-representation. A vector ξ ∈ H is cyclic for π if the orbit π(A)ξ = {π(a)ξ : a ∈ A} has dense linear span in H, i.e., H = closure(span(π(A)ξ)). In the GNS construction for a state φ, the vector ξ_φ = [1] (image of the identity in H_φ = completion of A/N_φ) is always cyclic. Every state on a C*-algebra arises from a cyclic representation via φ(a) = ⟨π(a)ξ, ξ⟩ (GNS theorem). A cyclic representation is irreducible if and only if the corresponding state is pure. Reference: Takesaki, Theory of Operator Algebras I, §I.9.
